name: Surface Code Intuition
summary: A practical way to picture large-scale error correction by spreading logical information across many physical qubits and repeatedly extracting syndromes.
whenToUseIt: Use it when reasoning about fault tolerance, error-correction overhead, and why useful quantum computing requires so many physical qubits.
steps: Separate data qubits from syndrome measurements conceptually.
Track how local checks reveal error patterns without measuring the logical state directly.
Interpret decoding as an inference problem over noisy events.
Relate physical error rate to threshold behaviour.
Estimate the logical cost of increasing reliability.
example: The surface code is attractive because it relies on local interactions, which align reasonably well with some hardware layouts even though the overhead is enormous.
